report_name: Mirai Variant 'xlabs_v1' Builds DDoS Botnet by Hijacking IoT Devices with Exposed ADB Ports

threat_actors.name: Tadashi
threat_actors.description: The operator of the xlabs_v1 botnet, a DDoS-for-hire service that targets IoT devices with exposed ADB ports.
threat_actors.goals: Monetization through a DDoS-for-hire service, with a focus on targeting gaming servers like Minecraft.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The xlabs_v1 botnet exploits the publicly accessible and often misconfigured Android Debug Bridge (ADB) service on TCP port 5555 to gain initial access to IoT devices.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The xlabs_v1 bot communicates with its C2 server, xlabslover.lol, using encrypted strings over web protocols.

attack_patterns.name: Match Legitimate Name or Location
attack_patterns.description: After infection, the xlabs_v1 malware renames its process to a common system name like '/bin/bash' to evade detection and avoid suspicion.

attack_patterns.name: Process Discovery
attack_patterns.description: The xlabs_v1 malware includes a 'killer' module that scans for and terminates processes belonging to competing malware or security tools to ensure it has exclusive control of the device's resources.

attack_patterns.name: Network Denial of Service
attack_patterns.description: The primary function of the xlabs_v1 botnet is to launch various types of DDoS flood attacks against specified targets, including specialized floods for gaming protocols.

malware.name: xlabs_v1
malware.description: A Mirai-based botnet malware that infects IoT and Android devices via exposed ADB ports (TCP/5555) to perform DDoS attacks as a for-hire service.

malware.name: Mirai
malware.description: The parent malware family from which xlabs_v1 is derived. Known for creating large-scale DDoS botnets from compromised IoT devices.

malware.name: VLTRig
malware.description: A Monero-mining toolkit found hosted on a server co-located with the xlabs_v1 infrastructure, suggesting a potential secondary monetization stream for the operator.

tools.name: Android Debug Bridge
tools.description: The development interface on TCP port 5555 that is left exposed on IoT devices and exploited by the xlabs_v1 botnet for initial access.

identities.name: Minecraft Servers
identities.description: A primary target for the DDoS attacks launched by the xlabs_v1 botnet, which includes specialized floods for the RakNet protocol used by these servers.

identities.name: Hunt.io
identities.description: The cybersecurity research firm that discovered the xlabs_v1 botnet's infrastructure.

infrastructure.name: xlabs_v1 C2 Server
infrastructure.description: The command and control server for the xlabs_v1 botnet, using the domain xlabslover.lol.

infrastructure.name: xlabs_v1 Staging and Distribution Server
infrastructure.description: A server located in the Netherlands (176.65.139.44, 176.65.139.9) used to host and distribute various compiled versions of the xlabs_v1 malware for different CPU architectures.

infrastructure.name: VLTRig Mining Host
infrastructure.description: A server (176.65.139.42) co-located with the xlabs_v1 infrastructure, hosting a VLTRig Monero-mining toolkit.

indicators.name: xlabs_v1 C2 Communication
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: xlabs_v1 Botnet C2 Communication
id: 2b1c8d3e-4f5a-4b6c-8d9e-0f1a2b3c4d5e
status: experimental
description: Detects network traffic to known C2 infrastructure associated with the xlabs_v1 Mirai-based botnet.
references:
  - https://blog.hunt.io/xlabs_v1-botnet-analysis-ddos-for-hire-minecraft/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-07
tags:
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.command_and_control
level: high
logsource:
  category: network_traffic
  product: zeek
detection:
  selection_dns:
    query: 'xlabslover.lol'
  selection_conn:
    id.resp_h:
      - '176.65.139.44'
      - '176.65.139.42'
      - '176.65.139.9'
  condition: selection_dns or selection_conn
indicators.description: Detects DNS queries for the xlabs_v1 C2 domain or direct network connections to its known staging, distribution, and co-located infrastructure IPs.

indicators.name: Exposed Android Debug Bridge (ADB) Port Access
indicators.pattern:
title: Exposed Android Debug Bridge (ADB) Port Access
id: 9e8d7c6b-5a4b-4c3d-2b1a-0f9e8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects network connections to the default Android Debug Bridge (ADB) port (TCP/5555), which is exploited by the xlabs_v1 botnet. This may indicate scanning activity or an active compromise attempt.
references:
  - https://blog.hunt.io/xlabs_v1-botnet-analysis-ddos-for-hire-minecraft/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-07
tags:
  - attack.t1190
level: medium
logsource:
  category: firewall
detection:
  selection:
    dst_port: 5555
    proto: 'tcp'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic targeting TCP port 5555, which is used by the Android Debug Bridge (ADB) and is the primary vector for xlabs_v1 infection.

relationships.source_name: Exposed Android Debug Bridge (ADB) Port Access
